rationale: Transaction type appears to be known at order creation, but business semantics should be confirmed before modeling.
derived_feature_guidance: May be used as a categorical candidate after train-only encoding.

rationale: Actual shipping duration is known only after fulfillment and can directly reveal late-delivery behavior.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for outcome audit, descriptive dashboards, and AO1 target validation.

rationale: Scheduled shipping duration is planned service information expected to be known before dispatch.
derived_feature_guidance: Can support planned-service features such as speed tier or urgent-service flag.

rationale: This field is an earnings/profit outcome and is documented as an exact duplicate or proxy of the AO2 target.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for AO2 target audit or descriptive historical reporting.

rationale: This field is order-value information but may duplicate Order Item Total and should not be included redundantly.
derived_feature_guidance: Prefer Order Item Total if both are available; document any selected financial predictors.

rationale: Delivery status is a post-delivery outcome and direct proxy for AO1 late-delivery target.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for descriptive reporting and target validation.

rationale: This is the official AO1 binary target and must never be used as a predictor.
derived_feature_guidance: Use as AO1 target only; exclude from predictor matrices.

rationale: Product category code is likely known at order creation but may be high-cardinality or duplicate Product Category Id.
derived_feature_guidance: Use directly only after categorical review; training-only encoding required for modeling.

rationale: Product category name is expected to be known at order creation and has clear business meaning.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be normalized or grouped deterministically before train-only encoding.

rationale: Customer city is likely known at order creation but may be high-cardinality and unstable.
derived_feature_guidance: Use normalized city or grouped region features; avoid blind one-hot encoding.

rationale: Customer country is a stable geographic field expected to be known at order creation.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be used as a regional categorical feature after train-only encoding.

rationale: Masked or direct customer contact information is an identifier and not operationally useful for modeling.
derived_feature_guidance: Exclude from modeling and dashboard outputs unless explicitly needed for data-quality audit.

rationale: Customer first name is a personal identifier.
derived_feature_guidance: Exclude from modeling and reporting artifacts.

rationale: Customer ID may be known at order creation but should not be used directly as a high-cardinality identifier.
derived_feature_guidance: May support historical aggregates only if computed with time-aware train-only logic.

rationale: Customer last name is a personal identifier and has limited modeling value.
derived_feature_guidance: Exclude from modeling and reporting artifacts.

rationale: Masked customer password/key is sensitive and non-operational for modeling.
derived_feature_guidance: Exclude from all modeling and dashboard artifacts.

rationale: Customer segment is expected to be known at order creation and has clear business meaning.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be normalized and encoded inside train-only preprocessing.

rationale: Customer state is expected to be known at order creation and supports regional analysis.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be normalized or grouped before train-only encoding.

rationale: Street-level address detail is high-cardinality and sensitive.
derived_feature_guidance: Exclude; use coarser regional fields instead.

rationale: Customer postal code may be known at order creation but is granular and partially missing.
derived_feature_guidance: Prefer availability flags or coarse geographic grouping; avoid raw zip as direct feature.

rationale: Department code is expected to be known at order creation but may duplicate department name.
derived_feature_guidance: Use with documented category/department grouping decisions.

rationale: Department name is expected to be known at order creation and has clear business meaning.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be normalized and encoded inside train-only preprocessing.

rationale: Latitude appears to describe customer/store geography and is expected to be known before dispatch, but precision should be controlled.
derived_feature_guidance: Prefer rounded coordinates or regional grouping for modeling.

rationale: Longitude appears to describe customer/store geography and is expected to be known before dispatch, but precision should be controlled.
derived_feature_guidance: Prefer rounded coordinates or regional grouping for modeling.

rationale: Market is an order destination operating region expected to be known before dispatch.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be used as a regional categorical feature after train-only encoding.

rationale: Order destination city is expected to be known before dispatch but may be high-cardinality.
derived_feature_guidance: Use normalized city, grouped geography, or training-only encoding with review.

rationale: Order destination country is expected to be known before dispatch and has clear business meaning.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be used as a regional categorical feature after train-only encoding.

rationale: Order customer code is an identifier likely known at order creation but should not be used directly as a raw high-cardinality feature.
derived_feature_guidance: May support training-only historical aggregates if approved.

rationale: Order timestamp is known at order creation and is needed for chronological split and seasonality features.
derived_feature_guidance: Use derived calendar features; avoid treating raw timestamp as an unconstrained model feature.

rationale: Order ID is a row/order identifier, not a predictive business signal.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for traceability, joins, deduplication, and validation.

rationale: Order-item product code is known at order creation but may be high-cardinality.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only after product grouping or train-only encoding review.

rationale: Discount value is likely known at order time but must be reviewed for AO2 target-reconstruction risk.
derived_feature_guidance: Allowed only if documented as pre-dispatch and not used to reconstruct profit target.

rationale: Discount rate is likely known at order time but must be reviewed for AO2 target-reconstruction risk.
derived_feature_guidance: Can support commercial structure features with AO2 review.

rationale: Order item ID is a row/item identifier, not a predictive business signal.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for traceability, joins, deduplication, and validation.

rationale: Product price is expected to be known at order time but may duplicate Product Price.
derived_feature_guidance: Prefer one price field only; document AO2 financial predictor selection.

rationale: Realized profit ratio can reconstruct or approximate AO2 target when combined with order value.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for descriptive historical margin audit, never as main AO2 predictor.

rationale: Item quantity is order composition information expected to be known at order creation.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be used directly as a numeric predictor after validation.

rationale: Gross sales is order-value information but may be mechanically tied to price and quantity.
derived_feature_guidance: Allowed only with documented AO2 financial predictor policy; avoid redundant value fields.

rationale: Discounted item/order value is expected to be known at order time and is recommended as AO3 denominator.
derived_feature_guidance: Conditionally allowed for AO2/AO3 with documented financial predictor policy.

rationale: This is the official AO2 target and must not be used as an AO2 predictor.
derived_feature_guidance: Use as AO2 target only; exclude from predictor matrices.

rationale: Order destination region is expected to be known before dispatch and has clear business meaning.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be normalized and encoded inside train-only preprocessing.

rationale: Order destination state is expected to be known before dispatch and supports regional analysis.
derived_feature_guidance: Can be normalized or grouped before train-only encoding.

rationale: Order status may encode cancellation, fraud, payment review, or fulfillment outcomes after order creation.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for descriptive analysis or AO1 population sensitivity decisions.

rationale: Order postal code may be known before dispatch but has high missingness and granular geography.
derived_feature_guidance: Prefer availability flags or coarse geographic grouping; avoid raw zip as direct feature.

rationale: Product code is known at order time but may be high-cardinality.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only after product grouping or train-only encoding review.

rationale: Product category code is known at order time but may duplicate Category Id.
derived_feature_guidance: Use one category representation and document the choice.

rationale: Product description is empty in the reviewed dataset and not useful for structured modeling.
derived_feature_guidance: Exclude from modeling; descriptive metadata only if populated in future data.

rationale: Product image URL/text asset is not part of the structured pre-shipment model.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for dashboard or catalog display if needed.

rationale: Product name is known at order time but may be high-cardinality.
derived_feature_guidance: Use normalized product name only after grouping or train-only encoding review.

rationale: Product list price is expected to be known at order time but may duplicate Order Item Product Price.
derived_feature_guidance: Prefer one price field only; document AO2 financial predictor selection.

rationale: Product stock/status indicator is expected to be known before dispatch but source semantics should be preserved cautiously.
derived_feature_guidance: Use neutral feature naming and document interpretation before modeling.

rationale: Shipment timestamp is known after order creation and may encode fulfillment timing.
derived_feature_guidance: Use only for operational reporting and outcome audit, not predictive features.

rationale: Shipping mode is selected/planned before dispatch and has clear operational meaning.
derived_feature_guidance: Can support shipping mode and speed-tier features.
